nodes.body: <<FILL role=setup words=210 beats='Vera Lindqvist boards the last northbound overnight train of the season as sleet turns to snow; the platform clock has already stopped; four ways to read this strange, half-empty train open before her'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=165 beats='Vera turns toward the night conductor and his silver punch-clock; the carriage sways and the cold glass holds her reflection over the dark; a first detail snags her attention'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=180 beats='the night conductor and his silver punch-clock offers Vera two ways to read it; one is direct and risks notice, the other is patient and lets the train keep its secret a while'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=150 beats='the direct approach to the night conductor and his silver punch-clock yields a hard, specific fact that will not sit still in Vera's mind; it points deeper into the train'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=175 beats='watching the night conductor and his silver punch-clock from the edges, Vera can either fold the observation into what she already knows, or let the late hour and the motion pull her under before she has understood it'>>

choices.label: Hold the thread and go on.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=160 beats='both readings of the night conductor and his silver punch-clock converge on the same small wrongness; Vera feels the journey close around it like a held breath'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=170 beats='carrying what the night conductor and his silver punch-clock has told her, Vera moves on through the swaying cars toward the question the whole train seems built around'>>

<<FILL role=setback words=150 beats='the pull of the night conductor and his silver punch-clock closes over Vera before she can hold it; she surfaces later having missed the moment, safe but emptied of the thing she almost understood'>>

<<FILL role=pivot words=205 beats='the first bottleneck, why the train never seems to arrive; every clock aboard has stopped and the north terminus stays exactly one hour off, no matter how long the night runs'>>

choices.label: Find the child who never sleeps.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=pivot words=205 beats='the second bottleneck, who the passengers really are; each traveller seems to be riding for a reason older than tonight, and none of them will say where they first got on'>>

<<FILL role=pivot words=205 beats='the third bottleneck, the choice at the mountain tunnel; the train slows before a black tunnel mouth in the peaks and Vera must decide what she will carry through the dark, and what she will leave'>>

choices.label: Ask the conductor for the whole truth.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=pivot words=205 beats='the passengers reveal themselves as the first grey light touches the peaks; Vera understands the train is a passage and not a route, and that how the night ends is now hers to shape'>>

choices.label: Ride on and simply be present.
Turn to the traveler who needs you.
Chase the last of the truth.
Try to get off before the tunnel.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=185 beats='Vera gives herself to the plain act of riding on, wide awake; the carriage narrows to this one long, lucid stretch of track before the tunnel'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=180 beats='in the plain act of riding on, wide awake there is a smaller door and a larger one; one closes the night early, the other stays open toward the grey dawn'>>

<<FILL role=resolution words=185 beats='having chosen the plain act of riding on, wide awake, Vera comes level with the dawn coming up over the ridgeline where all the night's threads seem to gather'>>
<<FILL role=setback words=150 beats='the calm Vera finds is real but slips through her fingers before the dawn; she arrives unsettled, sure only that she let a moment pass'>>
<<FILL role=resolution words=185 beats='Vera gives herself to the traveler at the carriage end who needs a hand; the carriage narrows to this one long, lucid stretch of track before the tunnel'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=180 beats='in the traveler at the carriage end who needs a hand there is a smaller door and a larger one; one closes the night early, the other stays open toward the grey dawn'>>

<<FILL role=resolution words=185 beats='having chosen the traveler at the carriage end who needs a hand, Vera comes level with the dawn coming up over the ridgeline where all the night's threads seem to gather'>>
<<FILL role=setback words=150 beats='Vera hesitates one beat too long and the hand she meant to take is withdrawn; the traveler rides on alone and so does she'>>

ending.title: The Hand You Did Not Take

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=185 beats='Vera gives herself to the last unread lines of the stranger's letter; the carriage narrows to this one long, lucid stretch of track before the tunnel'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=180 beats='in the last unread lines of the stranger's letter there is a smaller door and a larger one; one closes the night early, the other stays open toward the grey dawn'>>

<<FILL role=resolution words=185 beats='having chosen the last unread lines of the stranger's letter, Vera comes level with the dawn coming up over the ridgeline where all the night's threads seem to gather'>>
<<FILL role=discovery words=175 beats='Vera reads half the timetable's secret and no more; it is enough to know the train is kind, not enough to know why it chose her'>>
<<FILL role=resolution words=185 beats='Vera gives herself to the cold vestibule door and the idea of stepping down; the carriage narrows to this one long, lucid stretch of track before the tunnel'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=180 beats='in the cold vestibule door and the idea of stepping down there is a smaller door and a larger one; one closes the night early, the other stays open toward the grey dawn'>>

<<FILL role=resolution words=185 beats='having chosen the cold vestibule door and the idea of stepping down, Vera comes level with the dawn coming up over the ridgeline where all the night's threads seem to gather'>>
<<FILL role=setback words=150 beats='the door opens onto the first platform of all, and Vera wakes there as the train pulls away northward without her, the night unspent'>>

<<FILL role=pivot words=200 beats='every route through the night gathers at the dawn bottleneck above the tunnel; the peaks go gold, the passengers are quiet, and the last shape of Vera's journey waits on one final turning'>>

choices.label: Set the night down and stay.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=resolution words=190 beats='Vera stops trying to arrive; she lets the north terminus stay one hour off forever and finds, at last, that she does not mind the distance'>>
<<FILL role=choice words=180 beats='the seat beside the mourning traveler is warm; Vera can stay through the tunnel at the price of her own arrival, or step back and keep it'>>
<<FILL role=resolution words=190 beats='Vera speaks the north line's whole quiet truth aloud to the grey window, and the naming of it is its own kind of arrival'>>
<<FILL role=resolution words=185 beats='Vera asks the train for nothing but a safe waking, and the train, being kinder than she feared, grants exactly that and no more'>>

<<FILL role=success words=220 beats='the best ending: Vera rides the last train north with open eyes and an open heart, at peace with a journey that was never meant to end at a station; dawn holds and she is finally, wholly here'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=215 beats='Vera stays beside the mourning traveler through the tunnel and delivers her safely into the dawn; helping another home turns out to be the arrival Vera was riding toward all along'>>

<<FILL role=setback words=150 beats='Vera stays past every reasonable hour and the dawn slips by; the traveler is helped but Vera surfaces frayed and far from herself, the good deed shadowed by what it cost'>>

<<FILL role=discovery words=185 beats='Vera names the north line for what it is, a passage the season lends to travelers who are ready; she does not stay, but she leaves knowing, and the knowing settles something in her'>>

<<FILL role=setback words=165 beats='Vera wakes at her own quiet station, unharmed and a little haunted; the train is gone, the night unexplained, and she carries only the sense of a door she chose not to walk through'>>
